checklist_item.title: Verify Merkle Proof Construction: Does get_data_column_sidecars_from_block (SG-004/validator) use correct generalized index?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify Merkle proof targets blob_kzg_commitments field. 2. Verify generalized index is correct for Fulu fork BeaconBlockBody. 3. Validate proofs with verify_merkle_proof. 4. Cross-reference generalized index with SSZ spec for Fulu fork.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-41, Trust Boundary: tb-014. Incorrect generalized index makes all sidecars fail verification.

checklist_item.title: Verify Subnet Mapping: Is each sidecar published to correct gossip subnet?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar(column_index) produces consistent results. 2. Compare subnet mapping across multiple clients. 3. Verify each sidecar is published to exactly one subnet. 4. Identify any client that computes different subnet IDs.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-42, Trust Boundary: tb-010. Inconsistent subnet mapping fragments data availability.

checklist_item.title: Verify Sidecar Retention: Do validators retain sidecars for MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Request sidecars during retention period and verify serving. 2. Measure retention period and compare with MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS. 3. Request sidecars just before retention expires. 4. Identify any client with premature pruning.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-43, Trust Boundary: tb-012. Premature pruning reduces data recovery sources.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are values >= BLS_MODULUS rejected at bytes_to_bls_field?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Craft blob with byte sequences encoding values >= BLS_MODULUS. 2. Verify such blobs are rejected. 3. Compare behavior across multiple clients. 4. Verify no client silently reduces values modulo BLS_MODULUS.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-44, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Threat: stride-tampering-tb-001. Silent modular reduction causes consensus divergence.

checklist_item.title: Verify Proof Generation Performance: Does compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff (SG-019) meet time budget?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Measure O(n^2) proof generation time for single blob. 2. Measure proof generation for maximum blobs per block. 3. Compare total time with slot time budget. 4. Verify rate-limiting or resource allocation prevents DoS.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-45, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Threat: stride-dos-tb-001. O(n^2) complexity may exceed slot time.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trusted Setup Documentation: Is trapdoor compromise documented as single point of failure?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review trusted setup ceremony documentation. 2. Verify trapdoor compromise is documented as critical risk. 3. Verify ceremony protocol follows best practices. 4. Review multi-party computation transcript for completeness.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-46, Threat: stride-spoofing-tb-004. CONDITIONAL: Trusted setup compromise is foundational assumption. Discovery of ceremony weakness would be eligible.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does batch verification detect tampered evaluation data?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit cells with valid formatting but tampered evaluations. 2. Verify rejection by pairing-based verification. 3. Verify Fiat-Shamir challenge detects any individual cell tampering. 4. Test tampering in various positions within batch.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-48, Trust Boundary: tb-007, Threat: stride-tampering-tb-007. Batch verification must detect all tampering.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Can proofs be reused across different cells or commitments?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Take valid proof from one (commitment, cell) pair. 2. Attempt to use same proof for different cell or commitment. 3. Verify Fiat-Shamir challenge includes all components. 4. Verify substitution changes challenge r and invalidates verification.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-49, Trust Boundaries: tb-006, tb-007, Threat: stride-spoofing-tb-007. Proof reuse allows false data availability claims.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are mutually inconsistent recovery cells detected?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Provide partial cells that are individually valid but from different polynomials. 2. Verify recovery proceeds but post-recovery verification fails. 3. Verify incorrect recovery is not accepted without verification. 4. Identify any client missing post-recovery verification.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-50, Trust Boundaries: tb-008, tb-009, Threat: stride-tampering-tb-008. Inconsistent cells cause wrong polynomial recovery.

checklist_item.title: Verify Network Resilience: Can selective subnet censorship reduce availability below 50%?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Analyze custody group distribution across subnets. 2. Simulate targeted subnet disruption. 3. Calculate minimum number of subnets to censor for <50% availability. 4. Verify custody redundancy survives targeted censorship.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-51, Trust Boundary: tb-010, Threat: stride-dos-tb-010. Subnet censorship degrades data availability.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is sidecar request rate-limiting implemented?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Flood validator with sidecar requests. 2. Verify rate-limiting per-peer is implemented. 3. Measure resource impact on consensus duties. 4. Identify clients without rate-limiting on sidecar serving.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-52, Trust Boundary: tb-012, Threat: stride-dos-tb-012. Request flooding exhausts validator resources.

checklist_item.title: Verify Privacy: Can custody group membership be inferred from column serving behavior?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Request all columns from validator and measure response times. 2. Analyze availability patterns between custody and non-custody columns. 3. Identify timing or availability side-channels. 4. Verify response behavior is uniform across all columns.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-53, Trust Boundary: tb-012, Threat: stride-infodisclosure-tb-012. Custody inference enables targeted attacks.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are custody requirements computed from finalized state only?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify custody computation uses finalized or justified state. 2. Create fork scenario with different effective balances. 3. Verify custody assignments remain consistent across fork. 4. Identify any client using non-finalized state for custody.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-54, Trust Boundary: tb-011, Threat: stride-tampering-tb-011. Forked state causes custody divergence.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is blobs_bundle.proofs length validated before slicing?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify assertion: len(proofs) == CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB * len(blobs). 2. Submit blob bundle with incorrect proof count. 3. Verify rejection before proof slicing. 4. Verify no out-of-bounds access during slicing.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-55, Trust Boundary: tb-013, Threat: stride-tampering-tb-013. Missing validation causes crash during slicing.

checklist_item.title: Verify Merkle Proof Generalized Index: Is blob_kzg_commitments index correct for Fulu fork?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify generalized index for blob_kzg_commitments in Fulu BeaconBlockBody. 2. Compare across multiple client implementations. 3. Verify index is updated from pre-Fulu fork. 4. Validate Merkle proofs with correct index.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-56, Trust Boundary: tb-014, Threat: stride-tampering-tb-014. Incorrect index at fork causes network-wide disruption.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does incorrect inverse FFT produce higher-degree polynomials?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify inverse FFT with bit-reversal produces expected degree. 2. Compare coefficient degrees across client implementations. 3. Verify quotient polynomial stays within trusted setup bounds. 4. Test various blob inputs for degree consistency.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-57, Trust Boundary: tb-003, Threat: stride-elevation-tb-003. Higher-degree polynomials exceed trusted setup.

checklist_item.title: Verify Recovery Performance: Does recovery with minimum cells meet time bounds?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trigger recovery with exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB / 2 cells. 2. Measure recovery time with O(n log n) FFT operations. 3. Trigger repeated recoveries and measure resource impact. 4. Verify rate-limiting on recovery attempts.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-58, Trust Boundary: tb-008, Threat: stride-dos-tb-008. Repeated recovery exhausts resources.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is division by zero possible in recovery?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY is correct. 2. Verify coset does not overlap with FFT domain. 3. Verify zp_over_coset contains no zero values. 4. Test various missing cell patterns for division by zero.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-59, Trust Boundary: tb-008, Threat: stride-infodisclosure-tb-008. Incorrect root of unity causes crash.

checklist_item.title: Verify Data Availability Sampling: Is data withholding detectable?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Simulate data withholding attack. 2. Verify sampling by multiple peers detects withholding. 3. Analyze probability of evasion. 4. Verify economic incentives align with honest behavior.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-60, Trust Boundary: tb-010, Threat: stride-repudiation-tb-010. Successful withholding allows invalid state transitions.

checklist_item.title: Verify Custody Floor: Are low-balance validators assigned minimum custody groups?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify minimum VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT assignment. 2. Analyze impact of many low-stake validators on coverage. 3. Calculate required attack investment for coverage gaps. 4. Verify floor provides substantial baseline coverage.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-61, Trust Boundary: tb-011, Threat: stride-elevation-tb-011. Low-stake validators may dilute custody coverage.

checklist_item.title: Verify Bit-Reversal Consistency: Is BRP applied consistently across all code paths?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify BRP in polynomial_eval_to_coeff (SG-007). 2. Verify BRP in coset_shift_for_cell (SG-016). 3. Verify BRP in coset_for_cell (SG-017). 4. Verify BRP in recover_polynomialcoeff (SG-023). 5. Cross-reference all paths for consistency.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-63, Trust Boundaries: tb-003, tb-009. Inconsistent BRP causes intermittent proof failures.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is batch verification resource cost bounded?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Measure resource cost linear with num_cells. 2. Submit very large verification batches. 3. Verify maximum batch size is bounded. 4. Identify clients lacking batch size limits.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-64, Trust Boundary: tb-007, Threat: stride-dos-tb-007. Unbounded batches exhaust CPU.

checklist_item.title: Verify Fiat-Shamir Hash Security: Is hash_to_bls_field collision-resistant?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify hash function is cryptographically secure. 2. Verify random oracle behavior. 3. Attempt to find two input sets producing same challenge r. 4. Verify hash function is not weak or deprecated.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-65, Trust Boundary: tb-006, Threat: stride-spoofing-tb-006. Hash collision enables proof substitution.

checklist_item.title: Verify Pairing Check Soundness: Does e(LL, LR) == e(RL, [1]) reject invalid proofs?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify pairing check implementation correctness. 2. Submit invalid KZG proofs and verify rejection. 3. Calculate rejection probability (1 - 1/q). 4. Test pairing library for implementation bugs.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-68, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Unsound pairing allows forged proofs.

checklist_item.title: Verify Cryptographic Assumptions: Is BLS12-381 bilinear Diffie-Hellman assumption maintained?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review cryptographic assumption documentation. 2. Verify BLS12-381 is standard and well-analyzed. 3. Monitor for any mathematical breakthroughs in DLP. 4. Verify implementation uses correct curve parameters.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-69. CONDITIONAL: Foundational assumption. A practical DLP break would be a major mathematical breakthrough.
